violations.text: An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2025

1) Raw animal foods (shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (potatoes) in refrigerator #2. 2) Raw animal foods (shelled eggs, liquid eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (potatoes, roast beef) in refrigerator #4. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2025

Mayonnaise was observed held at 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. See disposal. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2025

The door to the outside, located at the entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025
Flour and drinks were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2025
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package in the kitchen area. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2025

The following were observed in poor repair: 1) The sanitizer dispenser in the ware washing area was not functioning properly and was unable to dispense sanitizer. 2) Refrigerator #5 door gaskets were observed torn. 3) Refrigerator #1 in the front area was in poor repair, ambient temperature observed at 48F. Equipment to be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 09/24/2025

Breakfast potatoes were observed held at 47-73 degrees Fahrenheit in the refrigerator. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, breakfast potatoes were left on the counter for about 30-45 minutes. Breakfast potatoes were placed on an ice bath to cool them during time of inspection. Establishment must not leave breakfast potatoes out of refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

Wood is used in the construction of shelves in the front counter area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024
Menus are lacking an allergy statement notifying customers of their obligation to notify staff if they have a food allergy New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer and ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food, debris and biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer must be cleaned. The ice machine was taken out of service during time of inspection and must not be used until it is cleaned. Ice was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

Cheese was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

Eggs and hamburgers are served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Sanitizer was disposed of and a new chlorine sanitizing solution was mixed to 50 ppm during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

An employee was observed prepping food in the 3-bay sink. Food must not be prepped in the 3-bay sink to prevent contamination from the premises. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

The ice scoop was observed with the handle touching the ice. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as ice must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

Wood is used in the construction of shelves in the front counter area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine and quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the sanitizing bucket and 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

Wood is used in the construction of storage shelves in the counter area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 07/21/2023
The ceiling in the kitchen areas are covered with wood that is not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 07/21/2023
